title: Customer Loyalty

jokes.text: The Holy Grail. Did you send Dwight on a quest for the Holy Grail? I think I'm a little too busy these days to send... Oh, my God, I did send Dwight on a quest for the Holy Grail.
jokes.setup: Jim acts too mature/busy for pranks now
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization he completely forgot about elaborate prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jim's evolution from prankster to responsible adult undermined by forgotten elaborate scheme. Great structure with false setup.

jokes.text: The Dunder Code! I completely forgot about that prank. That had to be, like, six or seven years ago. Stayed late every night for a month. Had a lot more free time back then.
jokes.setup: Jim remembering the prank details
jokes.punchline: Wistful comment about having more free time reveals life changes
jokes.explanation: Nice character development joke showing Jim's life progression, earned through established history.

jokes.text: Dwight climbing into ceiling above accounting while Jim and others watch
jokes.setup: Dwight following cryptic clues
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of adult man crawling through office ceiling
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comedy leveraging established character dynamics.

jokes.text: Man, I wish I was there to see his face when he gets to the end and finds the fake grail? No grail? You don't remember? I don't.
jokes.setup: Jim excited to see Dwight's disappointment
jokes.punchline: Jim completely forgot what his own prank's ending was
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Jim's anticipation undercut by his own forgetfulness, shows character growth/change.

jokes.text: What? That's a flush.
jokes.setup: Dwight reading cryptic clue 'Seat of entrance'
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact realization it means toilet
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Dwight's literal interpretation delivered with characteristic seriousness.

jokes.text: There's nothing down here. Oh. I expected more from Young Halpert. This just... Forget it, forget it.
jokes.setup: Dwight following elaborate treasure hunt
jokes.punchline: Disappointed critique of Jim's younger self's prank abilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - even when pranked, he critiques the execution quality. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, we created a fake profile for a really cool guy named Derek McBlack. Wow.
jokes.setup: Andy asks about social media initiative
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake name 'Derek McBlack' delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Solid absurd name humor, though somewhat predictable fake profile joke.

jokes.text: It's just Pete in sunglasses. Oh.
jokes.setup: Derek McBlack sounds like a cool mysterious person
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be obviously Pete with minimal disguise
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Derek McBlack setup, visual component adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: So far we're only popular with imaginary people, but we think this is the start of something big.
jokes.setup: Describing their social media success
jokes.punchline: Optimistic spin on having zero real followers
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic understatement about their complete failure presented as partial success.

jokes.text: Well, all right. Pete and I work well together. Not that there's anything special about Pete. It could be any guy. Or girl. Not that I'm into girls. Not that I'm into Pete. Ah! What was the question?
jokes.setup: Simple compliment about working with Pete
jokes.punchline: Spiraling over-explanation revealing her actual feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Erin escalation pattern - simple statement becomes increasingly flustered revelation.

jokes.text: Pam doing the 'Cece Spin and Kiss' dance move alone in talking head
jokes.setup: Pam describing cute dance move she taught Cece
jokes.punchline: Adult woman doing child's ballet move by herself on camera
jokes.explanation: Sweet visual comedy showing Pam's maternal pride, slightly awkward execution adds to charm.

jokes.text: 'Athlead.' Please. They're too lazy to call it Athletes Lead?
jokes.setup: Phyllis seeing Jim's company name
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of portmanteau as laziness
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay observation, fits Phyllis's character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Jim should just call it Stumpany for stupid company.
jokes.setup: Criticizing 'Athlead' name construction
jokes.punchline: Creates even worse portmanteau to make her point
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the wordplay joke, 'Stumpany' is memorably bad.

jokes.text: Well, take your worst fear and multiply it by infinity.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking when Jim's poaching will end
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dramatic mathematical response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's paranoia with absurd mathematical precision.

jokes.text: You won't stop until you've poached us all. Yeah. Even you. No! I'll be damned if I'm going to let us lose me.
jokes.setup: Dwight paranoid about Jim stealing employees
jokes.punchline: Vows to prevent himself from being stolen by Jim
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight logic - fighting against his own potential future betrayal of himself. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Potato? Oh, no. Kevin, it's not a joke.
jokes.setup: Oscar asking about Irish-American Cultural Center mural
jokes.punchline: Kevin assumes it's setup for Irish potato joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kevin's mind immediately goes to stereotypical joke setup.

jokes.text: I mean, who are they going to go with, some nobody like me, or a big name like Tracy Fleeb?
jokes.setup: Oscar being modest about his mural chances
jokes.punchline: Creates fake famous artist name on the spot
jokes.explanation: 'Tracy Fleeb' is a nicely absurd fake name, fits Oscar's tendency to name-drop.

jokes.text: Yes, Jim, I think I know how to point a rectangle at something.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Pam's phone video abilities
jokes.punchline: Reduces filming to most basic geometric description
jokes.explanation: Excellent reduction of technology to absurd simplicity, great defensive sarcasm from Pam.

jokes.text: Yeah, see? You've still got to press end, Pam. Press end.
jokes.setup: Pam claimed competence with phone
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves Jim's point by not hanging up properly
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback undermining Pam's confidence, good structure.

jokes.text: Alan Olifson from North Dakota. He also likes Hammermill and Georgia Pacific. Wow, that guy's really into paper. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Excited about their first real social media like
jokes.punchline: Guy only likes paper companies, obvious industry connection
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about their 'success' being another industry person.

jokes.text: Awkward handshake/fist bump choreography failure between Pete and Erin
jokes.setup: Celebrating their social media success
jokes.punchline: Botched celebration handshake creates awkward tension
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward physical comedy, serves relationship subplot but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Andy has just started to be nice to me and now I've sent his girlfriend into the arms of a younger man! And I cc'ed him on every incriminating memo. 'Pete and Erin, don't hold back. Our social media presence should be hot, hot, hot. Go at it vigorously.' I did everything but unzip their pants for them.
jokes.setup: Nellie realizes she's facilitated Pete/Erin relationship
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual interpretation of innocent business emails
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how innocent business language sounds suggestive in context.

jokes.text: Wait, this says 'Dunder Mifflin Loyalty Pledge.' This? Oh, what? Double mocha latte, please? You got it. Just sign your name below. Whatever you want.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering free coffee
jokes.punchline: It's actually a loyalty pledge Stanley doesn't read
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight scheme, good use of Stanley's coffee obsession overriding caution.

jokes.text: Customer loyalty. What is it? Can you hold it in your hand? Can you nudge it with your finger? Can you dump it on a woman? No! Why? Because it's an idea. But what does it mean?
jokes.setup: Dwight giving philosophical lecture on customer loyalty
jokes.punchline: Bizarre physical questions including 'dump it on a woman'
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - philosophical concept explained through increasingly weird physical actions.

jokes.text: Oh, it's when you get a free sandwich after you've already eaten 10 sandwiches. Not even close.
jokes.setup: Dwight's abstract question about loyalty meaning
jokes.punchline: Kevin's literal interpretation as punch card rewards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin response - reduces abstract concept to food-based transaction.

jokes.text: L... It. LoYa V Loyaw 'Loyalty' is exactly right!
jokes.setup: Dwight demanding Darryl say what loyalty starts with
jokes.punchline: Darryl struggles to pronounce 'loyalty' under pressure
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor of being put on the spot, works but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Let's all ask ourselves, have we been faithful in our relationships? Mmm. That's none of your damn business.
jokes.setup: Dwight's rhetorical question about faithfulness
jokes.punchline: Stanley's immediate shutdown of personal inquiry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley response - immediate boundary setting with characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: So is it all right for her to flirt with Creed, for example? Let's try it out. No, let's not say Creed. Let's say Mr. X.
jokes.setup: Dwight creating hypothetical about Erin flirting
jokes.punchline: Immediate backtrack from using Creed as example
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment recognizing Creed is too inappropriate even for hypotheticals.

jokes.text: Can we just say Pete, 'cause that's the guy that Erin's flirting with?
jokes.setup: Dwight's elaborate 'Mr. X' hypothetical
jokes.punchline: Kevin cuts through pretense with obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Great Kevin moment - cutting through elaborate setup with blunt obviousness.

jokes.text: Really? I'm sorry. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Pam gets important phone call during Cece's recital
jokes.punchline: Apologizing to other parents while staying on call
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor of social faux pas, serves story but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: I got the mural! Oh, my God! Um, I'm sorry, I have to go. My daughter is a ladybug. I know that doesn't make any sense. Um, thank you, thank you!
jokes.setup: Pam gets good news during inappropriate time
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation mixing professional and personal contexts
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - professional call interrupted by parental duties creates confused explanation.

jokes.text: You're very rude. Yes. Very rude. Kids are dancing!
jokes.setup: Pam disrupting dance recital with phone call
jokes.punchline: Escalating parental judgment and obvious statement
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe escalation, 'Kids are dancing!' is appropriately obvious.

jokes.text: So, should we go call Jim and go tell him to go screw himself?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to get Darryl to commit to Dunder Mifflin
jokes.punchline: Dwight's childish suggestion for confronting Jim
jokes.explanation: Fits Dwight's character - turning business rivalry into playground confrontation.

jokes.text: It can't be more fun than selling paper and paper products. It can. Are you pretending to be crazy?
jokes.setup: Dwight can't believe anything is more fun than paper sales
jokes.punchline: Darryl's blunt assessment of Dwight's mental state
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction - Dwight's paper obsession meets Darryl's directness.

jokes.text: Well, Andy's cute, but he's too vanilla. Whereas Pete, he's just one sick dude. I mean, you know this guy likes to get weird.
jokes.setup: Creed comparing Andy and Pete for Erin
jokes.punchline: Creed's completely wrong assessment of 'sick' Pete
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - completely misreading mild-mannered Pete as dangerous.

jokes.text: With slamming bods like that? They ain't playing checkers.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pete and Erin's relationship
jokes.punchline: Creed's crude assessment with weird metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed inappropriate comment with bizarre game metaphor.

jokes.text: People, it's 2013. Erin is a strong, independent woman. Who says she has to end up with any man?
jokes.setup: Phyllis making progressive feminist statement
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Erin doesn't need either man
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion - expected romantic advice becomes feminist independence statement.

jokes.text: Your feelings for Erin? Probably your heart. And a little bit your penis.
jokes.setup: Pete denying romantic feelings for Erin
jokes.punchline: Kevin's anatomically specific emotional analysis
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin bluntness, crude but fits character, somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Most relationships eventually die on their own. But sometimes they just need a little pillow over the face.
jokes.setup: Nellie deciding to break up Pete and Erin
jokes.punchline: Murder metaphor for ending relationships
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever metaphor, fits Nellie's manipulative character perfectly.

jokes.text: Yes, we will be delivering a shipment of paper today, but I will also be delivering you a big shipment of fun!
jokes.setup: Dwight forcing Darryl on fun delivery route
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top enthusiasm about 'delivering fun'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight energy, trying too hard to make work fun in his typical intense way.

jokes.text: Fun.
jokes.setup: Dwight's forced enthusiasm about fun delivery
jokes.punchline: Darryl's flat, unenthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word completely deflates Dwight's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I just remembered. I kissed that man.
jokes.setup: Angela discussing relationships with Oscar
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization about her past with Oscar
jokes.explanation: Good callback to awkward character history, perfect timing for realization.

jokes.text: It's 30 degrees out, you're drinking a milkshake? Nope. Fire in the hole! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Darryl questions Dwight's cold weather milkshake
jokes.punchline: Dwight throws milkshake into restaurant
jokes.explanation: Juvenile prank comedy, fits Dwight's misguided attempt at fun but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: You just threw a milkshake into a restaurant where they make minimum wage. It's a YouTube thing! Let's go, let's go! Come on. They're coming!
jokes.setup: Dwight's prank going wrong
jokes.punchline: Darryl's moral objection vs Dwight's YouTube defense
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between characters' moral perspectives, Dwight's YouTube excuse is appropriately tone-deaf.

jokes.text: That. I'll watch it. Let's get this over with. All right, you don't have to... Let's get this over with, Pam.
jokes.setup: Pam offering to show Cece's recital video
jokes.punchline: Meredith's aggressively unenthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith rudeness - making watching someone's kid video sound like punishment.

jokes.text: Oh, no! I took a phone call in the middle of taping and then when I went to turn it back on, I must have turned it off. User error. I've heard it happens to other people.
jokes.setup: Pam's earlier confidence about phone competence
jokes.punchline: Complete failure at the one thing she was confident about
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup, ironic failure of the 'simple' task.

jokes.text: I'm sure Pam is already kicking herself for choosing a phone call over her child.
jokes.setup: Pam's video failure explanation
jokes.punchline: Oscar's harsh judgment about parental priorities
jokes.explanation: Appropriately judgmental Oscar comment, cuts to the heart of Pam's guilt.

jokes.text: Whenever I tell him good news, he's always like 'Beesly!' I love that. The only thing better than getting the job. 'Beesly!'
jokes.setup: Pam excited about sharing mural news with Jim
jokes.punchline: Practicing Jim's reaction alone, including his pet name
jokes.explanation: Sweet but slightly sad character moment showing Pam's anticipation and relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: This barely even feels like a prank anymore.
jokes.setup: Jim watching Dwight still following the treasure hunt
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on the prank losing its humor
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about pranks losing impact over time, reflects Jim's character growth.

jokes.text: They make you come back and clean it up.
jokes.setup: Dwight proud of his milkshake prank
jokes.punchline: Practical consequence deflating the 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Darryl reality check - cutting through Dwight's childish excitement with practical consequences.

jokes.text: You never stop surprising me.
jokes.setup: Angela's awkward kiss realization
jokes.punchline: Oscar's awkward response to uncomfortable memory
jokes.explanation: Awkward but appropriate response to uncomfortable callback moment.

jokes.text: Did I just hear you laughing with glee? No. I was just clearing my throat.
jokes.setup: Dwight catching Creed enjoying the chaos
jokes.punchline: Creed's obvious lie about throat clearing
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed moment - caught enjoying others' misfortune, provides weak cover story.

jokes.text: Actually, funny story, I didn't get it. I shouldn't have been so cocky about my rectangle-holding skills after all.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for the recital video as a pick-me-up
jokes.punchline: Pam's self-deprecating callback to her earlier confidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with self-awareness, good use of established joke for character development.

jokes.text: Oh, great. So we'll see somebody else's kid with Cece in the background?
jokes.setup: Pam suggesting getting video from other parents
jokes.punchline: Jim's frustration about Cece being secondary in others' videos
jokes.explanation: Good parental frustration humor - the specific annoyance of background appearance in others' videos.

jokes.text: I mean, it's really not that hard to film a video.
jokes.setup: Jim's mounting frustration about missed recital
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about video complexity after Pam's failure
jokes.explanation: Builds tension for the relationship conflict, ironic given earlier phone debate.

jokes.text: Maybe you should have been there.
jokes.setup: Jim's criticism about the video failure
jokes.punchline: Pam's pointed response about his absence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation line - cuts to core issue with economy and emotional impact.

jokes.text: What am I doing wrong, Brian?
jokes.setup: Emotional moment after fight with Jim
jokes.punchline: Pam addressing camera crew member by name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment breaking documentary format - reveals crew relationship and character vulnerability.

jokes.text: Give her a minute. It's just a tough situation, all right? It's getting tougher.
jokes.setup: Documentary crew member speaking on camera
jokes.punchline: Revealing behind-scenes perspective and concern
jokes.explanation: Continues meta-documentary break, adds dramatic weight and outside perspective.

jokes.text: Let's turn the cameras off. Seriously, guys? Enough. Enough.
jokes.setup: Emotional breakdown moment
jokes.punchline: Complete break in documentary format for human compassion
jokes.explanation: Powerful format-breaking moment - documentary ethics vs human compassion, very bold for the show.

jokes.text: Fire in the hole! Go! Go! Go! I nailed that guy! I'm going to miss the paper business.
jokes.setup: Dwight repeating his milkshake prank
jokes.punchline: Pride in juvenile behavior while claiming to miss paper business
jokes.explanation: Repetitive physical comedy, saves it somewhat with the ironic 'miss paper business' line.

tmdb_overview: Dwight tries to stop Darryl from moving to Jim's start-up company. Jim misses his daughter's first recital, to Pam's dismay. Nellie spills the beans about Pete and Erin.
